Fisher Hill

Fisher Hill is a historic neighborhood within walking distance of the central business district of White Plains, NY.

Residential development began around 1900, and many of the large vintage homes from that era still define the neighborhood's character today.

Fisher Hill is notably diverse in ethnicity, age, and income.

items.title: About WPCNA
items.excerpt: Learn what WPCNA is, how it supports White Plains neighborhood associations, and how residents can contact the council.
items.text:
Learn what WPCNA is, how it supports White Plains neighborhood associations, and how residents can contact the council.

About

Our mission

“The White Plains Council of Neighborhood Associations coordinates the neighborhood associations in White Plains, NY. Its purpose is to enhance the quality of life in the City, and in particular, in its neighborhoods. As an advisory council, the WPCNA functions by disseminating information and opinion to the public and public officials, and by encouraging local action.”

Archived WPCNA mission statement

What WPCNA does

WPCNA is a forum for neighborhood associations across White Plains. Its role is practical: keep information moving, help associations stay connected, and support local action when neighborhood concerns need attention.

Share information

WPCNA helps circulate neighborhood concerns, meeting information, civic updates, and public-interest items that residents may otherwise have to find across several different sources.

Connect associations

The council gives neighborhood associations a place to exchange ideas, compare concerns, and learn from one another.

Encourage local action

When issues affect neighborhoods, WPCNA helps residents stay organized, informed, and connected to the right public process.

How this site helps residents

This site brings together the most useful WPCNA and community information in one place.

The goal is simple: make it easier for residents to see what is happening, understand where it fits, and find the right next step.

Neighborhood profiles and map-based orientation

Events and community activity

Meeting agendas and minutes

Community posting guidelines

CNA workshop materials for forming or strengthening a neighborhood association

How WPCNA works

Governance, by-laws, and committees

WPCNA’s by-laws and committee work help give the council structure. They support regular communication among neighborhood associations, provide a way to track issues, and help organize discussion around citywide topics.

If you are looking for governance materials, committee information, or guidance on how the council is organized, contact WPCNA and someone can point you in the right direction.

items.title: Community Posting
items.excerpt: How to submit community-relevant information to WPCNA for review and posting on the site.
items.text:
How to submit community-relevant information to WPCNA for review and posting on the site.

Community

Community posting

WPCNA shares community-relevant information from residents and local organizations. Here is how it works.

How community posting works

WPCNA accepts submissions from residents and community organizations for information that is relevant to White Plains neighborhoods. All submissions are reviewed before anything is posted or shared.

This is a curated, community-serving channel — not an open bulletin board. We share things that help residents stay informed about civic life, neighborhood activity, and public-interest events in White Plains.

What we share

Community events (school picnics, neighborhood gatherings, block parties)

Neighborhood initiatives (cleanups, safety walks, beautification efforts)

Civic and educational programs

City notices (hydrant flushing, road work, public hearings)

Sustainability and awareness campaigns

Local nonprofit and community organization announcements

What we don't share

Yard sales or personal listings

Business advertising or promotions

Commercial services or solicitations

Unreviewed or anonymous submissions

Everything posted through WPCNA should be community-serving, non-commercial, and relevant to White Plains residents.

How to submit

Use the Community Postings submission form with a clear subject line and the details you would like shared. Please include:

A brief description of what you are sharing

Relevant dates, times, and locations

A contact name and organization (if applicable)

Any links or attachments

WPCNA reviews all submissions. Not everything sent will be posted, and we may edit for clarity or length. If you have questions about whether something fits, just ask.

Submit

Send us your information

Community-relevant submissions can be sent through the review form. All posts are reviewed by WPCNA before sharing.

Guiding principle

Community-first

WPCNA is a civic resource, not a classifieds board. Everything we share should serve the broader community and help residents stay connected to neighborhood life in White Plains.

items.text:
In recognition of Asian Am erican Heritage Month, OCA- Asian Pacific Am erican Advocates - Westchester & Hudson Valley Chapter and the Westchester County…

Civic

Asian Am ericans and the Law

In recognition of Asian Am erican Heritage Month, OCA- Asian Pacific Am erican Advocates - Westchester & Hudson Valley Chapter and the Westchester County…

About this event

In recognition of Asian Am erican Heritage Month, OCA- Asian Pacific Am erican Advocates - Westchester & Hudson Valley Chapter and the Westchester County Human Rights Commission invite you to join us for "Asian Am ericans and the Law: An important discussion on civil rights challenges and triumphs for Asian Am erican communities throughout our nation's history," with featured speaker The Honorable Denny Chin. Denny Chin is a United States Circuit Judge for the United States Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit, the first Asian Am erican appointed to that circuit. He was confirmed in April 2010 and took senior status in June 2021. From 1994 until his appointment to the Second Circuit, he served as a United States District Judge for the Southern District of New York. Judge Chin and his wife Kathy Hirata Chin have written, produced, and presented, together with a team from the Asian Am erican Bar Association of New York, a series of reenactments of historic cases involving Asian Am erican litigants. Space is limited and registration requested. Register by calling 914-995-9500, email: Human Rights@Westchester CountyNY.gov, or visit: https://AAPI-Month-2026.eventbrite.com Check the library page for registration, tickets, and any schedule updates.

Learning

Creating a Compelling College Application this Summer

Program for High School Juniors: Creating a Compelling College Application this Summer We are offering this Zoom presentation to help high school juniors and…

About this event

Program for High School Juniors: Creating a Compelling College Application this Summer We are offering this Zoom presentation to help high school juniors and their parents understand how to use the upcoming summer productively to create a compelling college application. We will cover the college application process, including current admissions trends, writing the personal statement and supplemental essays, securing strong teacher recommendations, and understanding how students can position themselves to increase their chances of admission. We will also explore why a student's ability to express insights, experiences, and personal growth through their essays is more important than ever. Our presentation will include application strategies, such as the advantages and disadvantages of early decision, early action, and regular decision. We will review the factors students should consider when deciding whether to submit standardized test scores for test optional colleges and universities. We will also explain the different pathways through which students can be admitted to a particular school. We will examine the holistic admissions review process, what admissions officers consider important when evaluating applicants, and how students can develop a compelling narrative that strengthens their applications. We will also discuss the importance of community service and how it offers opportunities for personal growth and character development. Admissions officers place significant emphasis on a student's character and the ways they have contributed to their communities. This program provides a detailed and strategic overview of what students should focus on to make their college applications as compelling as possible. The presentation will be led by Ron Feuchs and Jackie Tepper, partners at Stand Out For College, LLC (www.standoutforcollege.com). Creating a Compelling College Application this Summer

Program for High School Juniors: Creating a Compelling College Application this Summer We are offering this Zoom presentation to help high school juniors and…

About this event

Program for High School Juniors: Creating a Compelling College Application this Summer We are offering this Zoom presentation to help high school juniors and their parents understand how to use the upcoming summer productively to create a compelling college application. We will cover the college application process, including current admissions trends, writing the personal statement and supplemental essays, securing strong teacher recommendations, and understanding how students can position themselves to increase their chances of admission. We will also explore why a student's ability to express insights, experiences, and personal growth through their essays is more important than ever. Our presentation will include application strategies, such as the advantages and disadvantages of early decision, early action, and regular decision. We will review the factors students should consider when deciding whether to submit standardized test scores for test optional colleges and universities. We will also explain the different pathways through which students can be admitted to a particular school. We will examine the holistic admissions review process, what admissions officers consider important when evaluating applicants, and how students can develop a compelling narrative that strengthens their applications. We will also discuss the importance of community service and how it offers opportunities for personal growth and character development. Admissions officers place significant emphasis on a student's character and the ways they have contributed to their communities. This program provides a detailed and strategic overview of what students should focus on to make their college applications as compelling as possible. The presentation will be led by Ron Feuchs and Jackie Tepper, partners at Stand Out For College, LLC (www.standoutforcollege.com). Check the library page for registration, tickets, and any schedule updates.

Workshop

Digital Decluttering

In this workshop, we'll slow down and actually understand why digital chaos builds up - and what to do about it.

About this event

In this workshop, we'll slow down and actually understand why digital chaos builds up - and what to do about it. You'll walk away with a clear framework, a few simple strategies to start right away, and a whole lot less dread about opening your laptop. Shannon Reilly is the founder of Simplify Space & Soul, a holistic wellness and organizing practice dedicated to helping people feel more at ease in their spaces, their schedules, and themselves. She holds certifications as a Professional Organizer (CPO, CVPO), Neurodiversity Coach, and Productivity Specialist, and has been teaching Therapeutic Yoga for over a decade. For more than ten years, Shannon has helped people cut through overwhelm - whether that shows up as a cluttered home, an overflowing inbox, a packed calendar, or just the feeling that life is moving too fast to catch up with. Her work is always customized, never judgmental, and rooted in the belief that small, sustainable changes make the biggest difference. This program takes place on Zoom and registration is required. Click here to register. The program is being presented in partnership with the Ruth Keeler Memorial Library and several other Westchester Libraries. Born in Missouri to enslaved parents just two weeks before the Civil War ended, Carver was one of the most prominent black scientists of the 20th century. Join us for some fun by talking about Carousel Summer by Kathleen Gros!

Learning

Graphic Novel Chat & BINGO: Carousel Summer by Kathleen Gros

Join us for some fun by talking about Carousel Summer by Kathleen Gros!

About this event

Join us for some fun by talking about Carousel Summer by Kathleen Gros! The first 4 students to register will receive a free copy of the graphic novel. Upon registration, please come to the Trove reference desk to pick up your free copy of the graphic novel. Other participants can have a copy placed on hold for them. For best discussion results, please make sure to read the graphic novel before coming to the program. We will also be playing a BINGO game based on Carousel Summer and snacking on treats. About Carousel Summer: With her best friend away at camp, tons of chores to do, and her dad always on her case for being such a tomboy, Lucy is dreading summer. That is, until Milforth's plan to revive an old carousel for the town's 150th anniversary brings artist Ray and her daughter, Anaïs, to town. Anaïs is smart, funny, and easy to talk to, and Lucy--who's used to being judged for her looks and interests--finally feels at ease in her own skin. And she thinks she may feel something for Anaïs, too. Leading up to Milforth's big birthday, tensions begin rising with locals, thanks to a shifty development company trying to overrun the town. Things also come to a breaking point at home, when Lucy butts heads with her dad over how she wants to express herself as a girl. Can Lucy find the courage to be true to who she is? She's got the whole summer to find out. Check the library page for registration, tickets, and any schedule updates.

About the Book Noodle & Bao: Momo has lived in Town 99 her entire life. She knows all its quirks and rhythms: the best places to buy fruit, practice tai-chi in the park, and, most of all, get the best meal: Noodle & Bao, run by Momo's best friend, Bao, and their am ah, Noodle. But Town 99 is changing. Rent is becoming unaffordable for Momo and her parents, and even Noodle & Bao has been edged out of its storefront, which was just recently bought out by a new business venture--Fancé Cafe. Fancé is run by the am bitious Ms. Jujube and her henchmen, who claim they're only beautifying Town 99 with good business. Momo knows that's not true, and knows that if she doesn't do something, she'll lose everything she loves about her neighborhood. items.text:
Join us for some fun by talking about Shiny Misfits by Maysoon Zayid!

Learning

Graphic Novel Chat & BINGO: Shiny Misfits by Maysoon Zayid

Join us for some fun by talking about Shiny Misfits by Maysoon Zayid!

About this event

Join us for some fun by talking about Shiny Misfits by Maysoon Zayid! The first 4 students to register will receive a free copy of the graphic novel. Upon registration, please come to the Trove reference desk to pick up your free copy of the graphic novel. Other participants will have a copy placed on hold for them. For best discussion results, please make sure to read the graphic novel before coming to the program. We will also be playing a BINGO game based on Shiny Misfits and snacking on treats. About the Book Shiny Misfits: Bay Ann wants to shine. No matter what. She's sure her moment in the spotlight has arrived when she wins the school talent show with a show stopping tap routine! But then her classmate and crush, Alyee Maq, causes her to wobble and almost fall. The video of him catching her goes viral, making Alyee an overnight sensation for "helping her." Bay Ann is reduced to her disability and her talent is ignored. Bay Ann doesn't want her classmate to get all the fame, and she is NOT satisfied being anything but the best. She'll do everything in her power to beat Alyee at his own attention-seeking game. With the help of her two best friends, Michelle and Davey Matt, she'll go up against Alyee and his crew to prove she's number one. But as Bay Ann tries to find the thing that really makes her stand out, everything she tries goes disastrously wrong. What if the only way to beat her enemy. is to join him? Check the library page for registration, tickets, and any schedule updates.

items.excerpt: Pack your (imaginary) bags and join musical educator Rebecca Schoffer for a joyful, interactive sing-along celebrating Jewish Am erican Heritage Month!
items.text:
Pack your (imaginary) bags and join musical educator Rebecca Schoffer for a joyful, interactive sing-along celebrating Jewish Am erican Heritage Month!

Learning

Over the Rainbow and Through the Woods

Pack your (imaginary) bags and join musical educator Rebecca Schoffer for a joyful, interactive sing-along celebrating Jewish Am erican Heritage Month!

About this event

Pack your (imaginary) bags and join musical educator Rebecca Schoffer for a joyful, interactive sing-along celebrating Jewish Am erican Heritage Month! Travel through time with songs that journeyed across oceans and generations, from traditional Yiddish folk to Am erican classics, beloved children's music, and pop hits by Jewish Am erican songwriters. Families will clap, move, and sing together, as we explore how music tells stories of home, hope, and the Jewish Am erican experience. Recommended for families with children ages 0-8. ~~~~ Rebecca Schoffer is an experiential educator, musician, and community builder who travels to organizations around the world to lead joyful concerts and teach educator workshops. She serves as Director of Jewish Family Life and the leader of the beloved Shababa community at the 92nd Street Y. She is the founder and creative director of Glowhouse Studio, an independent production house. Her latest musical family film, FLAME, premiered in 2025. She is also a recording artist and singer-songwriter who has released multiple albums of Jewish family music, available everywhere you stream music. A graduate of the Atlantic Theater Conservatory and Tufts University, she is a playwright and director of family theater. She received the 2019 Young Pioneers Award for innovative educators. Learn more at: www. Rebecca Schoffer.com Connect on Instagram: @Rebecca Schoffer Music Watch on You Tube: https://www.youtube.com/@The Rebecca Show Check the library page for registration, tickets, and any schedule updates.

items.title: Rev250: Revolutionary Women
items.excerpt: Westchester County is rich in Revolutionary history.
items.text:
Westchester County is rich in Revolutionary history.

Civic

Rev250: Revolutionary Women

Westchester County is rich in Revolutionary history.

About this event

Westchester County is rich in Revolutionary history. Although known as the "Neutral Ground," many of the important military decisions, strategic planning, and major events took place on the streets we walk every day. You've all heard stories about General George Washington and local hero John Jay. These men's efforts and great accomplishments are well documented and well known. However, did you know that many local women also made significant contributions to the revolutionary cause? Are the names Ann Fisher Miller and Phebe Ward familiar to you? Let us gather to tell the stories of these brave local women and the significant impact they had in our fight for independence. In honor of Am erica's 250th anniversary, please join us at the White Plains Library on Saturday, June 13th, 2026 at 2:00pm as we honor these brave women patriots! Learn about their lives and roles during the Am erican Revolution. Listen as an historical interpreter takes us back to 1777 and shares the legend of Sybil Ludington's ride. Join us in a fun-filled game of trivia (with prizes!) This event is free and light refreshments will be served. Check the library page for registration, tickets, and any schedule updates.

About this event

Local historian Stephen Paul Devillo will discuss his latest book, Westchester County in the Am erican Revolution: Neutral Ground. "After the 1776 Battle of White Plains, Westchester County became known as the 'Neutral Ground.' But there would be no peace here. Occupied by neither side, it was a place where no one could remain neutral am id clashes by detachments of the contending armies, as well as violence by groups of bandits, vigilantes and partisans. Stephen Paul De Villo details the colorful personalities and diverse military units that shaped the course of the conflict" (Publisher's website). Learning

REVOLUTIONARY SOUNDS!

Revolutionary Sounds explores the rich cultural exchange between European and African music in colonial New York.

About this event

Revolutionary Sounds explores the rich cultural exchange between European and African music in colonial New York. Through live performance, storytelling, and hands-on demonstrations, students are introduced to historical instruments like the mountain dulcimer, djembe, banjo, and shekere. The program also highlights the significance of Pinkster, one of the earliest African Am erican holidays, and how music served as a vital thread connecting communities across cultures. With engaging songs, body percussion, and interactive call-and-response segments, Revolutionary Sounds brings history to life while illuminating the roots of Am erican music traditions like jazz and rock and roll. #RW250 Check the library page for registration, tickets, and any schedule updates.

Civic

Short Story Film Discussion Series

Join White Plains Librarian Barbara Wenglin to explore compelling readings from the rich anthology, Music Stories (ed.

About this event

Join White Plains Librarian Barbara Wenglin to explore compelling readings from the rich anthology, Music Stories (ed. Wesley Stace, Everyman's Pocket Classics, 2024). These vibrant and varied works by diverse authors capture the universal - sometimes controversial -- world of music, paying tribute to one art form through another. We will also discuss related film selections at some sessions, to view before we meet. For this session: Film Discussion - CODA (2021 - 1hr. 50min.) Oscar winner: Best Picture, Best Supporting Actor, Best Screenplay Title means Child of Deaf Adults, and three of the actors are deaf, using Am erican Sign Language in real life. High school daughter Ruby is a talented singer and the only hearing person in the family. Encouraged by her music teacher, she is torn by her desire to attend music conservatory and helping her parents as their lifelong interpreter. With stellar performances by Marlee Matlin, Emilia Jones, Daniel Durant, and Oscar winner Troy Kotsur. To view before session via DVD in catalog, also streamed on Apple TV, Am azon Prime Video. We will meet Thursdays from 2:00 - 4:00 p.m on 6/25. Meeting ID for all sessions is: 837 1709 4842 or phone in at 1-929-436-2866, no password needed. Click This Zoom Link to register once for the full series and join us when you can. The program is supported by The Friends of the White Plains Library. All are welcome, including newcomers! About this event

Join Jeffrey from Inclure, a program developed by the Harris Project & WJCS that brings teens together for fun, supportive, and wellness-centered programming. Each week, we'll explore different ways to improve physical, mental, and emotional well-being. Whether it's through stress management techniques, light movement, or guided breathing exercises, teens will develop skills to build confidence, foster positive relationships, and enhance their overall sense of well-being. For teens in grades 7-12 on a first-come, first-served basis. Check the library page for registration, tickets, and any schedule updates.

items.excerpt: The recent movie adaptation of Emily Bront&euml;'s novel Wuthering Heights has brought renewed interest to the enduring classic.
items.text:
The recent movie adaptation of Emily Bront&euml;'s novel Wuthering Heights has brought renewed interest to the enduring classic.

Learning

Wuthering Heights Tea Party & Discussion

The recent movie adaptation of Emily Brontë's novel Wuthering Heights has brought renewed interest to the enduring classic.

About this event

The recent movie adaptation of Emily Brontë's novel Wuthering Heights has brought renewed interest to the enduring classic. Her gothic story of thwarted love and the resulting vengeance wreaked over two generations continues to enthrall readers. Brontë passed away at age 30, leaving Wuthering Heights as her only novel. Please join us in "Rotary Room Heights" for tea, discussion, and to celebrate this masterwork. Many people have used this novel as a creative springboard, and toward the end of our time we will do a little informal creating also. Registration is required. Check the library page for registration, tickets, and any schedule updates.

Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated Pi Iota Omega Chapter is seeking students ages 10-13 (all genders) to participate in the Alpha Kappa Alpha Youth Leadership Institute (YLI). items.excerpt: Anastasia: The Musical is the spectacular new musical about discovering who you are and defining who you’re meant to be.
items.text:
Anastasia: The Musical is the spectacular new musical about discovering who you are and defining who you’re meant to be.

Arts

Anastasia: Youth Edition

Anastasia: The Musical is the spectacular new musical about discovering who you are and defining who you’re meant to be.

About this event

Anastasia: The Musical is the spectacular new musical about discovering who you are and defining who you’re meant to be. From Tony winners Terrence Mc Nally, Stephen Flaherty and Lynn Ahrens, creators of such Broadway classics as Ragtime and Once On This Island, this dazzling show transports its audience from the twilight of the Russian Empire to the euphoria of Paris in the 1920s, as a brave young woman sets out to discover the mystery of her past. Pursued by a ruthless Soviet officer determined to silence her, Anya enlists the aid of a dashing con man and a lovable ex-aristocrat. Together, they embark on an epic adventure to help her find home, love and family. $1350 (Early Bird) $1500 (Regular) Use the show page for tickets and the full performance schedule.

School Of Rock: Young Actors

Andrew Lloyd Webber’s School of Rock: Young Actors Edition is a dynamic, one-hour version of the musical based on the hit movie.

About this event

Andrew Lloyd Webber’s School of Rock: Young Actors Edition is a dynamic, one-hour version of the musical based on the hit movie. The tuner follows Dewey Finn, a failed, wannabe rock star who decides to earn an extra bit of cash by posing as a substitute teacher at a prestigious prep school. There he turns a class of straight-A pupils into a guitar-shredding, bass-slapping, mind-blowing rock band. But can he get them to the Battle of the Bands without their parents and the school’s headmistress finding out? Please note this version of the show does NOT require any of the actors to play instruments. $1350 (Early Bird) $1500 (Regular) Use the show page for tickets and the full performance schedule.

A practical guide from WPCNA's workshop materials for residents starting, reviving, or strengthening a neighborhood association.

Join the CNA

A practical starting point

WPCNA supports residents who want to build stronger neighborhood organizations in White Plains. This handbook grows out of last year's workshop and is meant to be a practical guide for people who are starting fresh, reviving an older association, or figuring out what organized neighborhood work looks like in real life.

The material below walks through the basics: building a contact list, setting a regular meeting rhythm, working with the City, and staying connected to other neighborhood groups through WPCNA.

Workshop guide and slides

Full presentation deck from the March 11, 2025 workshop, covering organizing basics, sample agendas, and practical templates.

Download slides (PDF)

Workshop flyer

The original flyer with the workshop date, location, and agenda.

Download flyer (PDF)

Use it with WPCNA

If you are thinking about starting a neighborhood association, this is a good first resource. Use it to get oriented, then reach out to WPCNA if you want help talking through the next step, finding out how other associations are organized, or getting connected to the wider council.

White Plains history

A short guide to historical threads connected to White Plains neighborhoods and civic life.

Scope

Historical context in the guide

The current site is mainly a civic guide, not a full local history archive, but some neighborhood pages already point to important historical threads in White Plains.

Those threads include the Battle of White Plains, long-standing institutions and campuses, and neighborhoods with roots in the 1700s, early 1900s, and 1950s.

The events calendar also picks up history and genealogy programs when current partner sources publish them.

Learn about Fisher Hill, part of West Side and Near-Downtown Neighborhoods.

Fisher Hill

Fisher Hill is a historic neighborhood within walking distance of the central business district of White Plains, NY. Residential development began around 1900, and many of the large vintage homes from that era still define the neighborhood's character today. Fisher Hill is notably diverse in ethnicity, age, and income.

Safe Streets Bicycle Visibility Clinic

Jun 12, 2026

Volunteers will provide free bicycle light installation, helmet fitting guidance, and visibility tips for riders.

A curated community resource

This page is for local information that serves White Plains residents and neighborhoods.

WPCNA reviews submissions before anything is posted. The goal is to share useful civic, neighborhood, and community updates — not classifieds, advertising, or personal listings.

Submitting an item does not guarantee it will be posted. If it fits WPCNA’s guidelines, it may be shared on the site, circulated through the council, or used to help residents stay informed.

What’s welcome

Community-serving information

Neighborhood events, school picnics, cleanups, and local meetings

Civic or educational efforts, including awareness campaigns and newsletters

City-related notices such as hydrant flushing, road work, or public-interest updates

Local initiatives that help residents stay informed or get involved

What we don’t post

Not classifieds or advertising

Yard sales, personal listings, or private services

Business advertising, promotions, or commercial offers

Unreviewed announcements or anything that must appear immediately

Items unrelated to White Plains residents or neighborhood life

What to include

Please include the basics residents will need: what you are sharing, who it is for, when it happens, where it takes place, and any link WPCNA should use if the item is posted.

Keep your message brief and clear. If WPCNA needs more information, someone may follow up.

Submission form

Send something for review

Use this form to send community information to WPCNA. Submissions are reviewed before posting.

Your email address is used for follow-up and will not be published unless you specifically ask WPCNA to include it.
